hypothesis_en: Mr President, I agree that a European growth initiative should be promoted, but I cannot accept the restrictive meaning given to the so-called Quick Start List. There is already a list of priority projects endorsed by the European Councils of the ESN and Dublin and a second extension is proposed by the Commission. The challenge should not be to reduce the number of projects but to put them into operation as soon as possible. High-speed railway sections such as the Vitoria DAC could be well executed by 2006 and yet they are not included in the QuickStar list, and I also believe that the so-called sea highways must be considered as cross-border projects as long as they connect ports of two Member States.
reference_en: thank you very much indeed Mr President I do agree that we've got to have a European Growth Initiative we've got to foster that but nevertheless (1.04) why be so restrictive in the way we're looking at this the list of priority projects which have been approved by the Essen Dublin summits and also the Commission for for a second enlargement (1.55) all these priorities should have the same access and possibilities for community funds th= there shouldn't be the challenge to reduce the number of projects but to actually implement as many as possible as quickly as possible that that'd be more effective to (1.01) have more money brought in this as soon as possible for for these ends w= and (1.59) so for the next three years we have authori= we have certification of projects that have actually being carried out they should be able to get money quickly rather th= rather than any other method these r= railway projects I think are very good for two thousand and six but they're not actually involved in the list and and those r= motorway projects should be seen as transfrontier projects rather than being classified in any other way rather than between two free ports
source_es: muchas gracias señor Presidente yo estoy de acuerdo con que se impulse una iniciativa europea de crecimiento sin embargo no puedo aceptar el sentido restrictivo que se ha dado a la conocida como Quick Start List existe ya una lista de proyectos prioritarios avalados por los Consejos Europeos de Essen y Dublín y está propuesta por la Comisión una segunda ampliación yo creo que todos estos proyectos prioritarios deben tener las mismas posibilidades de acceso a las ayudas comunitarias el reto no debe ser reducir el número de proyectos sino ponerlos en marcha cuanto antes considero sin ninguna duda que sería más eficaz el habilitar una prima un plus de financiación a los proyectos o tramos de los mismos cuyas certificaciones de obra ejecutada se presenten en los próximos tres años por ejemplo y no reducir a priori la lista de proyectos en base a sus expectativas de inicio o de realización tramos de ferrocarril de alta velocidad como el Vitoria-Dax podría ejecutarse en buena medida de aquí al dos mil seis y sin embargo no está incluido en la Quick Start List y también considero que las denominadas autopistas del mar tienen que ser consideradas como proyectos transfronterizos siempre que unan puertos de dos Estados miembros muchas gracias señor Presidente
